name: Oeufs a La Neige
recipeIngredient: 1 Vanilla bean, split and seeds scraped out
recipeInstructions:
---------------------------------MERINGUES---------------------------------
1 cup Low-fat milk
4 large Egg whites
1/4 cup Sugar
"For this low-fat meringue treat (its name translates as "eggs in the snow"), we've made a creme anglaise that calls for low-fat milk instead of cream, and two whole eggs instead of as many as eight egg yolks."
==========
FOR SAUCE: Combine milk and orange peel in a heavy medium saucepan. Scrape in seeds from vanilla bean; add the bean. Bring the mixture to a simmer. In a separate bowl, combine sugar and cornstarch. Whisk in eggs. Gradually add hot milk mixture, whisking constantly. Return the mixture to the saucepan. Cook over medium heat until the mixture is almost thickened enough to coat a spoon, stirring constantly (about 9 minutes); do not boil. Strain into a shallow bowl and refrigerate until well chilled, at least 2 hours.
FOR MERINGUES: Place milk in a heavy large skillet. Add enough water to reach about 1-1/2 inches depth. Bring the liquid to a gentle simmer. Beat egg whites in a medium bowl until soft peaks form. Gradually add sugar and beat until meringue is stiff but not dry. Using two spoons dipped into cold water, form puffs from half of the meringue mixture, dropping each puff into the simmering liquid. Poach for 4 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, transfer meringues to a kitchen towel. Repeat with remaining meringue. Arrange meringues atop custard and chill. (Can be made up to 4 hours ahead.)
